myDinoParkCodes.title: My Dino Park Codes
myDinoParkCodes.subtitle: Redeem active My Dino Park codes for Cash, Teeth, and the Frozen Egg before buying paid eggs.
myDinoParkCodes.intro: Use these codes after the tutorial opens the Store menu. Redeem the full active list first, then hatch the free egg and spend Cash on early dinosaurs.
myDinoParkCodes.redemptionHint: Launch My Dino Park on Roblox, finish the tutorial, open Store, scroll to Redeem a gift code, paste the code, then press Enter or Redeem.

myDinoParkBeginnerGuide.title: My Dino Park Beginner Guide
myDinoParkBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Start by unlocking the Store, redeeming codes, hatching your first dinosaurs, and reinvesting early income into more attractions.
myDinoParkBeginnerGuide.intro: My Dino Park’s opening loop is simple: buy Dino Eggs, hatch dinosaurs, attract visitors, then reinvest earnings into more dinosaurs and income upgrades. Follow the steps in order so early Cash turns into income quickly.

steps.title: Launch the Roblox experience
steps.description: Open My Dino Park from its Roblox experience page and let your park fully load.
steps.result: You enter your starter plot and can begin the tutorial flow.

steps.title: Finish the tutorial first
steps.description: Complete the short tutorial before redeeming codes or buying eggs.
steps.result: Core menus such as Store, eggs, placement, and park actions become usable.

steps.title: Redeem all working codes
steps.description: Open Store, scroll to the gift-code box, then redeem UPDATE2, BESTDINOGAME, DINOSLOVESU, WELCOME, and THX4PLAYING.
steps.result: You receive early Cash, Teeth, and the Frozen Egg before spending starter Cash.

steps.title: Buy or hatch the first egg
steps.description: Start with the Common Egg when spending Cash; it costs 500 Cash and hatches instantly.
steps.result: Your first dinosaur starts generating visitor interest right away.

steps.title: Place every new dinosaur
steps.description: Use your inventory, place hatched dinosaurs on open land, and avoid leaving eggs idle.
steps.result: More visible dinosaurs means more visitor income feeding back into the park.

steps.title: Fill land before chasing expensive eggs
steps.description: Use Common and Uncommon Eggs to fill early space, then move toward Rare Eggs once your Cash flow is stable.
steps.result: You build a reliable income base before saving for bigger purchases.

steps.title: Move into Rare and Epic value
steps.description: Target Rare Eggs at 10,000 Cash, then Epic Eggs at 25,000 Cash after your park has enough earners.
steps.result: Velociraptor, Stegosaurus, Brachiosaurus, Triceratops, and Spinosaurus become your main growth targets.

steps.title: Buy income upgrades after the park has dinos
steps.description: Use Visitor Booth at 2,000 Cash as the first upgrade, then Food Court, Dino Hotel, and Helicopter Tour as your income grows.
steps.result: Upgrades multiply visitor income after you already have enough dinosaurs worth multiplying.

myDinoParkTierList.title: My Dino Park Tier List
myDinoParkTierList.subtitle: Rank dinosaurs, eggs, and spending priorities by early value, income, rarity, and update relevance.
myDinoParkTierList.intro: Filter by progression stage or item type before spending. Starter picks build income quickly, mid-game picks improve Cash per minute, late-game picks anchor the park, and code-only rewards should be claimed before paid purchases.

entries.name: Frozen Egg
entries.reason: Free egg value plus 10,000 Cash from the same code makes it the strongest first-session reward.

entries.name: T-Rex
entries.reason: Highest listed dinosaur income and the main late-game park anchor.

entries.name: Legendary Egg
entries.reason: Best end-game egg because it leads to T-Rex income.

entries.name: Helicopter Tour
entries.reason: The strongest income multiplier after your park already has strong earners.

entries.name: Spinosaurus
entries.reason: Top Epic income and a strong bridge into late-game.

entries.name: Triceratops
entries.reason: Stable Epic-tier income with strong visitor appeal.

entries.name: Epic Egg
entries.reason: Best mid-to-late game egg before saving for Legendary.

entries.name: Dino Hotel
entries.reason: Doubles visitor income once the park has enough dinosaurs to justify it.

entries.name: Brachiosaurus
entries.reason: Best Rare-tier listed income and a strong crowd attraction.

entries.name: Stegosaurus
entries.reason: Reliable Rare-tier income with good value.

entries.name: Velociraptor
entries.reason: Best early upgrade target once Common and Uncommon dinosaurs are producing.

entries.name: Rare Egg
entries.reason: Main mid-game egg and the first big jump over starter income.

entries.name: Food Court
entries.reason: Strong income boost after you have several dinosaurs placed.

entries.name: Pterodactyl
entries.reason: Solid early income and useful variety before Rare Eggs.

entries.name: Ankylosaurus
entries.reason: Good filler when building early income density.

entries.name: Parasaurolophus
entries.reason: Low-investment Uncommon option before the Rare Egg jump.

entries.name: Uncommon Egg
entries.reason: Best early upgrade after the first Common Egg batch.

entries.name: Visitor Booth
entries.reason: First multiplier upgrade once basic dinosaur slots are filled.

entries.name: Dilophosaurus
entries.reason: Best Common-tier listed income, useful while starting out.

entries.name: Gallimimus
entries.reason: Cheap early filler that helps start the visitor loop.

entries.name: Compsognathus
entries.reason: Lowest-cost dinosaur for opening income.

entries.name: Common Egg
entries.reason: Correct first purchase because it is cheap and instant.

myDinoParkEggsGuide.title: My Dino Park Eggs Guide
myDinoParkEggsGuide.subtitle: Compare egg costs, hatch times, rarity pools, hatch results, and best buying moments before spending Cash.
myDinoParkEggsGuide.intro: Eggs are the core spending decision in My Dino Park. The usual route is Common into Uncommon, Rare once Cash flow is stable, Epic for mid-to-late growth, and Legendary after the park can recover from the 50,000 Cash spend.

eggs.bestUse: First purchase and early land filling.
eggs.buyTiming: Buy immediately after tutorial or after redeeming starter codes.
Buy after several Common dinosaurs are placed and earning.
Buy once early Cash flow is stable; Velociraptor is a strong first Rare target.
Buy after Rare dinosaurs and basic upgrades are already supporting the park.
Buy when the park can rebuild Cash quickly after a 50,000 Cash spend.

eggs.bestUse: Free early hatch before spending Cash on paid eggs.
eggs.buyTiming: Redeem DINOSLOVESU during the first session, then hatch it before buying higher-cost eggs.

myDinoParkMoneyGuide.title: My Dino Park Money Guide
myDinoParkMoneyGuide.subtitle: Build income by hatching dinosaurs, attracting visitors, completing quests, redeeming codes, upgrading your park, and expanding land. The clean spend order is eggs first, then stronger dinosaurs, then park upgrades, then land expansion.
myDinoParkMoneyGuide.intro: My Dino Park uses a simple tycoon loop: buy eggs, hatch dinosaurs, place them in your park, attract visitors, collect Cash, and reinvest into a bigger dinosaur collection. Teeth are used as a premium-style progress currency and are currently most visible through update rewards and quest progress, while Cash powers the main park growth path.

steps.title: Redeem active codes before buying eggs
steps.description: Start with the free rewards from the Store code box, then use the Cash to speed up early egg purchases. Codes are case-sensitive, so enter them exactly as posted.
steps.action: Open My Dino Park, press Store, scroll to Redeem a gift code, enter the code, and claim the reward.

steps.title: Fill empty space with affordable eggs first
steps.description: Early income depends on having dinosaurs placed, not on saving for a dream T-Rex while your park sits empty. Buy Common-tier eggs first so every open space starts producing visitor income.
steps.priority: Use Common Egg dinosaurs to start the income loop, then move into Uncommon and Rare eggs once the park has steady Cash flow.

steps.title: Hatch and place every useful dinosaur
steps.description: Visitors pay to see dinosaurs, so newly hatched dinosaurs should be placed quickly. A placed low-tier dinosaur is better than an empty slot while you are still building your first income base.
steps.cashSource: Visitor income from dinosaurs placed in visible park spaces
steps.upgradeRule: Keep cheap dinosaurs until you can replace them with a higher-income hatch.

steps.title: Use quests as the steady bonus path
steps.description: Quests support the main park loop by giving extra progress while you buy eggs, hatch dinosaurs, and expand. Clear them alongside normal play instead of treating them as a separate chore.
steps.cashSource: Quest rewards and park progress tasks
steps.teethSource: Quest progress and update reward paths

steps.title: Replace weak dinosaurs with Rare earners
steps.description: Once your park is producing, stop overbuying the lowest tier and start replacing weak dinosaurs with higher-income species. Velociraptor is the strongest early upgrade target because it gives a major jump in income without needing endgame Cash.

steps.title: Buy park upgrades after the dinosaur base is working
steps.description: Park upgrades multiply the value of a park that already has dinosaurs. Buy them after you have a stable set of placed dinosaurs, not before, because an empty upgraded park adds no income on its own.

steps.title: Expand land only when your current land is productive
steps.description: Land expansion is strongest after your current spaces are filled with earning dinosaurs. Expand, fill the new space with eggs, replace weak dinosaurs, then repeat the loop.
reinvestmentOrder: Buy eggs to fill empty spaces
Hatch and place dinosaurs for visitor Cash
Replace low earners with Rare, Epic, and Legendary dinosaurs
Buy park upgrades once income is stable
Expand land and repeat the cycle

myDinoParkDinosaursList.title: My Dino Park Dinosaurs List
myDinoParkDinosaursList.subtitle: Search every standard dinosaur by rarity, source egg, unlock method, income value, and replacement priority. Use this list to decide which dinosaurs to keep, replace, or chase next.
myDinoParkDinosaursList.intro: The standard collection currently spans 12 dinosaurs across Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic, and Legendary rarity tiers. Higher-rarity dinosaurs cost more to hatch but bring much stronger visitor income, so the best collection path is to fill the park early, then replace low earners with stronger species.

dinosaurs.visitorValue: Best park attraction and highest standard income
dinosaurs.replacementNote: Endgame keeper; replace only for duplicates or future stronger limited dinosaurs
Keep until Legendary upgrades are affordable
Keep as a strong mid-to-late game earner
Replace only after Epic dinosaurs become affordable
Good stable earner before Epic upgrades
Keep through early-mid game; replace with Epic dinosaurs later

dinosaurs.visitorValue: Good early variety and filler income
dinosaurs.replacementNote: Replace with Rare dinosaurs when park slots become valuable
Use as a bridge into Rare eggs
Replace after your Rare egg income loop starts
Replace with Uncommon or Rare dinosaurs once Cash flow improves
Use early, then replace as soon as higher-tier eggs are affordable
Good only for filling empty early space; replace quickly

myDinoParkUpdateLog.title: My Dino Park Update Log
myDinoParkUpdateLog.subtitle: Track the latest My Dino Park update, active codes, new eggs, balance changes, and bug fixes. The current Roblox title marks the game as UPD 2.
myDinoParkUpdateLog.intro: Use this log for the details players actually need: what changed, which codes were added, what rewards matter, and whether there are new eggs, dinosaurs, currency changes, or restore-related notes.

updates.title: Update 2 — New Codes & Bug Fixes
updates.summary: Update 2 adds three new reward codes, introduces a Frozen Egg through code redemption, and includes bug fixes, server stability improvements, and Teeth economy adjustments.
newContent: Frozen Egg added as a code reward through DINOSLOVESU
currencyChanges: UPDATE2 adds both Cash and Teeth rewards
DINOSLOVESU adds a Frozen Egg and Cash reward

updates.title: Update 1 — Game Launch
updates.summary: The launch version introduced the core park tycoon loop: buy eggs, hatch dinosaurs, attract visitors, earn Cash, upgrade the park, and grow toward the top park ranking.
newContent: 12 standard dinosaur species across Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic, and Legendary tiers
balanceChanges: Initial dinosaur income values and egg progression
currencyChanges: Cash established as the main park growth currency
Teeth established as a secondary progress currency

myDinoParkOfficialLinks.title: My Dino Park Official Links
myDinoParkOfficialLinks.subtitle: Use the official Roblox experience, Dino Community group, and Discord invite for the safest path to play, get codes, find restore help, and follow updates.
myDinoParkOfficialLinks.intro: This module keeps the player-facing links clean: Roblox for playing the experience, Dino Community for the official group, and Discord for announcements, codes, restores, and community support. No third-party wiki, Trello, or aggregator link is included here.

links.title: Official Roblox Experience
links.label: Play My Dino Park
links.description: Launch the official My Dino Park Roblox experience by Dino Community. The game page lists the current UPD 2 title and the core loop: buy Dino Eggs, hatch Dinosaurs, attract visitors, upgrade your park, and grow toward the top park rank.

links.title: Dino Community Roblox Group
links.label: Open Dino Community
links.description: Open the official Roblox community connected to My Dino Park. Use it as the group-side source for developer identity, community access, and Roblox-native updates.

links.title: Official Discord
links.label: Join Discord
links.description: Join the official My Dino Park Discord for codes, announcements, update news, restore help, and player discussion.

links.title: Restore Help
links.label: Get Restore Help
links.description: Use the official Discord path for restore support. The Roblox experience description directs players to official links for restores, so this is the clean route instead of trusting mystery links.

links.title: Code Sources
links.label: Check Official Code Sources
links.description: Use the official Discord and Roblox-linked community sources for new code drops. Current active rewards include UPDATE2, BESTDINOGAME, DINOSLOVESU, THX4PLAYING, and WELCOME.

links.title: Wiki and Trello
links.label: Use Official Links Only
links.description: No third-party wiki or Trello link is shown in this module. Players should use only links posted through the official Roblox experience, Dino Community group, or official Discord.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about My Dino Park

questions.question: What is My Dino Park?
questions.answer: My Dino Park is a Roblox dinosaur park tycoon by Dino Community where you buy eggs, hatch dinosaurs, attract visitors, earn Cash, and upgrade your park toward the #1 Dino Park rank.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in My Dino Park?
questions.answer: Finish the tutorial first, then open Store, scroll to Redeem a gift code, paste an active My Dino Park code, and press Enter or Redeem to claim your rewards.

questions.question: Which eggs should I buy first in My Dino Park?
questions.answer: In My Dino Park, start with the Common Egg at 500 Cash to fill space quickly, then move into Uncommon and Rare Eggs as your Cash flow stabilizes, and save for Epic and Legendary Eggs once your park earns reliably.

questions.question: What are Teeth used for in My Dino Park?
questions.answer: Teeth are a secondary progress currency in My Dino Park, currently most visible through update code rewards and quest progress, while Cash powers the main park growth path.

questions.question: How many players can join a My Dino Park server?
questions.answer: A My Dino Park server supports up to 6 players at once, so you build your dinosaur park in a small shared experience rather than a single huge lobby.

questions.question: Where can I find new My Dino Park codes?
questions.answer: New My Dino Park codes are posted through the official Dino Community Discord and the Roblox experience page, so follow both to catch every code drop right after updates.

cta.title: Ready to Build Your Dino Park?
cta.description: Join the My Dino Park community on Discord for codes, guides, updates, and discussions!
